description: Trio resilience layer (FEAT-010 slice 4): wrap the per-spec body in run_trio_pass (planner/orchestration.py, the TRIO_SEQUENCE loop calling _run_one_spec + save_version) so a per-spec failure does NOT abort the 14-spec run. Bounded retry with machine-readable feedback, then degrade-to-recorded-entry + continue. Retryable: transient LLM errors (LLMTimeoutError/LLMRateLimitError, retry unchanged) + parse/catalogue ValueError (re-render with the rejection message appended as steering, up to cap R, default 2). Non-retryable (immediate degrade, no retry): LLMAuthenticationError + fold shape errors (TypeError/ValueError from the seam folds -- a parser-bug signal). On exhaustion/non-retryable: record a DegradedSpec (spec_key, attempts, reason, severity=MUST_FIX), skip that spec's fold/save (contributes nothing), continue. run_trio_pass gains a 4th return value: the list of DegradedSpec. Cascade-tolerant (later specs see 0 prior rows gracefully, already true). DegradedSpec is a RETURNED structure (Flag-MODEL-row persistence is slice #9; the gate-closure re-invoke is #8). Realizes DEC-57 dec-4 + DEC-58 + the user's no-aborts requirement. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=resilience.
acceptance_criteria: A per-spec failure (LLM error, parse/catalogue rejection, or fold error) does NOT abort run_trio_pass; the run completes and returns, with the failing spec recorded rather than raising.

Retryable failures retry up to a bounded cap (default 2): a parse/catalogue ValueError re-renders the spec's prompt with the rejection message appended as steering before the next attempt; a transient LLM error (timeout/rate-limit) retries the same prompt. A spec that succeeds on a retry folds normally and is NOT recorded as degraded.
Non-retryable failures (LLM authentication error; a fold shape error TypeError/ValueError) degrade immediately with no retry.

After exhausting retries (or immediately for non-retryable), the spec degrades to a recorded entry (spec_key, attempt count, reason, severity) and the loop continues; the degraded spec contributes nothing to draft_state.

run_trio_pass returns the list of degraded specs alongside the accumulated draft_state; a degraded prior spec does not crash a later spec (cascade-tolerant -- the later spec sees the missing contribution gracefully).

The LLM calls remain logged; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates. (Flag-model-row persistence and the promotion-gate-closure re-invoke are slices #9/#8, out of scope here.)
